calendar.matters:
Petition For Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Jul-12-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Amanda Ebey, via ZOOM, for Charles Andrews, via ZOOM, Petitioner; Chris Land, via ZOOM, for Lura Sims, Objector; Sydney Hall, via ZOOM, for Theodore Bennett, Petitioner.

The court begins by advising counsel that the court does not see a basis to support venue in San Francisco or any other county within California as the only trust asset is in Texas where Mr. Andrews resides and intends to administer the trust in Texas. The court reminds counsel that aside from the venue issue, the court may not have in rem jurisdiction over this trust as the principal location of administration is nonexistent in any location within California. The court also reminds counsel that if there is no trustee, code section 17500(b) requires a petition to appoint a trustee in the county in which the trust property is located. Ms. Ebey reports that she is seeking an order from this court which would grant disbursement of trust funds from a blocked account to Mr. Andrews.

After additional discussion, the Petition for Appointment of Successor Trustee is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Vedica Puri. Probate Examiner: Sandra Hilton. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Loo. Not reported.

Petition For Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar May-25-2021 Continued To Jul-07-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar May-11-2021 Continued To May-25-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar May-05-2021 Continued To May-11-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Christopher Land appeared by zoom for Lura Sims (Respondent/Objector).
Amanda Ebey appeared by zoom for Charles Andrews.

The Court stated that a request for dismissal was filed by Theodore Bennett on April 7, 2021, that Mr. Bennett's petitions (Petitions for an Order to Confirm Successor Trustee (2)) are off calendar and that Ms. Sims has to file her own petition if she wants to be appointed as the Successor Trustee. The Court also stated that it sees no basis for justifying venue in San Francisco, that Mr. Andrews resides in Texas and that the assets are in Texas. Ms. Ebey stated that she understands that, that her Client has already been appointed as the Temporary Trustee, that she is asking for an extension of that for her Client to deal with the $300,000.00 after discovered assets and that then the Court will no longer have jurisdiction. The Court stated that the problem is that the reason for granting appointment of the temporary trustee was an exigent circumstance. Ms. Ebey raised the issue of the Court not even granting an ex parte petition for appointment of a temporary trustee without having a petition to appoint a Trustee and stated that the money sitting in escrow is exigent. The Court informed Ms. Ebey that it is leaning against it and that she needs to provide a complete copy of the trust. Ms. Ebey stated that all of the documents have been provided, that the issue raised by the Examiner of the missing pages is incorrect, that exhibit E is the Revocable Appointment Trust 2000 and that exhibit D is the will exercising the power of appointment. The Petition for Appointment of Successor Trustee is under submission.

Judge: Vedica Puri. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Filing Of Proof Of Deposit In Blocked Account
Off Calendar Apr-13-2021 Off Calendar

Petition For An Order To Confirm Successor Trustee; Confirm Assets To Revocable Trust And For Order Compelling Respondent To Account For Trust Assets
Off Calendar Apr-13-2021 Off Calendar

Petition For An Order To Confirm Successor Trustee; Confirm Assets To Revocable Trust And For Order Compelling Respondent To Account For Trust Assets
Off Calendar Apr-13-2021 Off Calendar

Petition For Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Mar-23-2021 Continued To Apr-13-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sydney Hall appeared by zoom for Theodore Bennett (Petitioner).
Amanda Ebey appeared by zoom for Charles Andrews (Respondent).
Lura Sims (Interested Party) appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.

The Court questions why the funds from the proceeds of the sale are being held in an account in Texas. Ms. Ebey stated that this was a sale of a property that had been held by family members in Texas with many Beneficiaries, that this trust was entitled to a percentage of it and that they were told by Chicago Title Company that because her Client was a Temporary Trustee, they would hold the funds. Ms. Ebey also stated that the Principal (Clayton) of the title company has confirmed that the funds are there, that the funds are in a protected account and that they are available for distribution. In addition, Ms. Ebey stated that she does not think the funds will be moved to California because her Client lives in Texas and that once the funds are received, jurisdiction of this trust will not remain here. The Court informed Ms. Ebey that a full copy of the trust is still needed. Ms. Ebey stated that she thought a copy of the trust was attached to her pleadings, that she can provide a full copy and that yesterday she received a request for dismissal from Mr. Hall for his petition. Ms. Sims expressed concern stating that she wants to make sure the order does not provide the Trustee with any authority over the Randall Andrews Jackson Trust B, that there is a Bank of America account with $235,000.00 in the Randall Trust and that she wants to be sure that there will be no authority to transfer funds from Randall's trust to Cora's trust. The Court stated that there will be no order on the Randall Trust, that it is not making an order on the issue that Ms. Sims has mentioned and that the trust that Ms. Sims is talking about is not before the Court. The Petition for Appointment of Successor Trustee, the Petition for An Order to Confirm Successor Trustee, etc. (2) and status on filing of the receipt for the blocked account are continued to April 13, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The order to appear is off calendar.

Judge: Vedica Puri. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Off Calendar Jan-19-2021 Continued To Feb-16-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sydney Hall appeared by telephone for and with Theodore Bennett by zoom.

The Court noted that it has not received any filings since the December 4, 2020 order. Mr. Hall stated that he has not received any status information. Mr. Bennett stated that the Texas matter was closed, that the title company was supposed to put the funds in escrow and that he has not received any information. An Order to Appear will be issued to the Trustee Charles Andrews and the Attorney Amanda Ebey to appear on February 16, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear and why they have not been provided proof of the blocked account. The status hearing is also continued to that date.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.
